8 min read
Just now

--

Transformers have become the foundation of modern Artificial Intelligence. From ChatGPT and Claude to Gemini, Llama, Mistral, DeepSeek, and countless domain-specific language models, nearly every breakthrough in Generative AI traces back to the Transformer architecture introduced in the landmark paper Attention Is All You Need.
As a result, interview expectations have changed dramatically. A few years ago, it was enough to explain self-attention, positional encoding, and the encoder-decoder architecture. Today, especially for Machine Learning Engineer, AI Engineer, Applied Scientist, Research Engineer, and Generative AI roles, interviewers increasingly ask conceptual and scenario-based questions that go far beyond textbook definitions.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Md Ishak Rahman on UnsplashThese questions are designed to evaluate how deeply you understand transformer architectures, their strengths, limitations, scaling behavior, and the trade-offs involved in building and fine-tuning large language models.
In this article, we’ll explore fifteen advanced transformer interview questions and discuss the concepts interviewers expect experienced candidates to understand.
1. Why Do Transformers Scale So Well with Data and Parameters, While RNNs…
